channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS… — 70 replies — 280.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS…

Open thread • Replies: 70 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:55 UTC

OP

NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!!

THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!!

SOMEONE MUST PUT A STOP TO THIS NOW!!!!!!!!!!!! REDDIT ARMY RISE UP!!!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62375140
this is entirely sustainable, you have EDS if you disagree

62375150
>>62375140
Short it then, stupid nigger

62375163
>company called "Space Exploration Technologies"
>run by the first trillionaire

>you STILL didn't buy any of their stock

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62376337
>>62375128
Are goys allowed to sell yet?

62376364
>>62375755
Satellites alone would be worth multiple trillions.

62376848
shorted at 220

feels good man

62377060
>>62375393
Whatever nigger, he kept promising the Hyperloop, the robotaxis and Mars for next year since 2015.

>"But buh reddit"

Curious how Elon was the guy being praised by Onions individuals in reddit before he allied with Trump, now he is praised by le based onions individuals like you.

62377191
>>62375818
SpaceX isn’t even a space company. It’s an AI company (and underperforming shit AI) with a rocket hobby. You also only get access to 4% of the stock on retail. You’re begging for a giga rugpull.

62377238
>>62375128
AND A ZIONIST!!

62377697
>>62375128
hm, congrats to the buyers.

62379451
>>62375140
mathematically impossible valuation.

62379484
>>62375255
Your instinctive contrarianism is why you belong to the permanent underclass

62379507
>>62375841
Anti-natalists are mentally ill and project their retardation onto others.

62379519
>>62377191
tbf I like grok more than gemini, copilot, or deepseek. Not sure yet how I think it stacks up to claude or chatgpt though.

62381795
>>62375140
how to gotted so much dollars?

62381800
>>62375128
Imagine believing 2 weeks represents absolutely anything about the viability or future trajectory of a stock. No wonder you guys get annihilated so regularly.

62381830
>>62377191
SpaceX performs 60% of the world's space launches, which is 95% of the American market. If they aren't a space company, then there isn't a space company that exists at all.

62381991
>>62381830
>He thinks (((outer space))) is like in the movies and what they teach in (((schools))) and show on your (((television)))
Cute. You need to be 18 to post here, champ.

62382075
>>62375140
>>62375255
Unlike faggy AI, space is an actual next frontier of human development.
While indians are busy generating piles of poo here on earth to jerk off to, real MEN will be mining asteroids in space and speculating on the Asteroid Belt stock exchange

62382118
my parents aped in and told me they didn't want to get left behind lol. Load zee 60% dump.

62382338
>>62382075
>real MEN will be mining asteroids

62382719
>>62382075
>Unlike faggy AI, space is an actual next frontier of human development.

SpaceX won't fucking EXIST by the time we get there.

62382732
>>62382118
That's really sad :(

62382961
>>62379484

62384212
>>62375140
Musk haters BTFO

People fail to realize that it's not just SpaceX. It's X.com, it's xAI, It's X payments. It's literally everything internet related. People can hate on Musk all they want, but he will own the internet and all internet related activity including purchasing, social media will go through his companies. Enjoy staying poor

62384226
>>62384212
>X.com, it's xAI, It's X payments
so it's facebook. although I like xAI, half of their stuff is good on the AI scale but sourcing data from twitter is a mistake.

62384237
>>62384226
It could have been Zuck, but he's straight up unlikable and wasted so much time and money on the wrong tech (VR) while everyone else moved to AI

62384247
Lol @ this thread
Elon is a weird incel

62384277
SpaceKeks

62384281
>>62375128
OP ARE YOU OK??

62384293
kwab

62384467
>>62381830
SpaceX is an AI company. Rockets are a literal hobby when you look at the specifics of the valuation. It’s like buying a house in order to get the Twinkie sitting inside the fridge.

62384596
bought at 170 sold at 203 how low it can go before I slurp back in

item.title: > age 34 > drained down to last $20k — 57 replies — 228.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
> age 34 > drained down to last $20k

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:57 UTC

OP

> literally impossible to get just a fucking interview these days
> once had $160k nw (circa 2022) but combination of awful timing decisions with crypto + 4 years unemployed did a toll on me with living expenses
> entrepreneurship is hypersaturated beyond belief, impossible to make money online now
> AI is slaughtering every money making field out there so I can't figure out which masters degree or cert I need to go for next and they're all so fucking expensive too

I'm so scared bros HELP

Early replies (first 3)

62382538
>unemployed 4 years
lmao, imagine being this worthless

62382547
>>62382527
just think if you had bought chip stocks instead of fucking around with crypto you would probably be a millionaire.

62382548
>>62382538
fuck off nigger ur probably some 19 year old shithead u dont know anything life will rape u and humble u faggot
>>62382527
Dont worry about it anon im sure u can find a decent job soon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382669
>>62382655
Buy an ad stinkie

62382670
>>62382662
>goy cattle
You think the Jews are sitting around all day NEETing playing bing bang wahoos?
No nigga they're charging Cletus 30% interest for a 72 month loan on his King Ranch.

62382672
>>62382561
Should've lifted with your knees

62382673
>>62382670
you wouldn't know, best to stay in your lane

62382675
>>62382673
>you wouldn't know
I know more than you.

62382678
>>62382672
>knees don't blow out
>it is scolio
no lifting form could have ever prevented this.

62382681
If not a shill demoralizing thread, get CDL and get a local hourly LTL job. It’s easy money once you learn how to use the truck safely. Also includes just the right amount of physicality so you won’t be a fat ass

62382683
>>62382675
>t. retard.

62382688
>>62382683
I'm right.

62382695
>>62382688
>t.

62382735
>>62382527
if you think you are fucked with 20k left i really hope you run down to 0 so you see how good you actually have it now

62382754
WHO CARES NIGGUH IRAN WON WOOOO

62382755
>>62382548
nah you could have gotten a job in 4 years you're just stupid and will remain poor for life.

62382778
>>62382670
Yeah. Jews literally just Neet and collect welfare. That is unironically what Hasidic jews do.

62382792
>>62382527
>33 years old
>6'3"
>190 pounds
>Agent 47 Chad facial features
>6 pack abs
>$720k net worth
>Live in the middle of nowhere
>Ohio
>Virgin

62382803
>>62382612
Whatever gaylord, entrepreneurship isn’t for cowards like you

>>62382648
Is this OP? If so, >>62382653 is right.
You also had 160k to figure something out.
What are your skills? If you’re tech savvy at all, why not use AI for something to sell like an app or two? Why not make AI porn profiles on social media?
What networking events did you attend?

62383084
>>62382538
>>62382631
You fuckers want a job? Isn't the whole point of this board getting rich enough to NEET eternally

62383305
>>62382648
>then 2023 AI reared it's ugly
Nigga you dum as hell. No one was laid off in 2023 because of AI. Probably the numbers in 2026 are still 4 digits globally.

62383334
>>62382648
learn chinese nigga. i work for chinese companies. in tech, the salary is amazing and you get white privelege. sucks to not have a niche. i got another offer for even more money yesterday just through guanxi

62383336
>>62382792
Kek

62383357
>>62383336
It's true, lol

62383834
>>62383084
He who climbs a mountain does not plant his flag at the foothills.

62383838
I'm 34 years old and I just absolutely drained my balls.

62383842
>>62382527
Use that $20k and buy a pressure washer

62383876
>>62383084
No you're supposed to find something that pays you to do something that you enjoy so you can take speculative bets without hurting too much

62384161
>>62382527
I was in the exact same position. Moved back home, and spent all day self teaching and filling out 1200 job applications. After 4 years unemployed I made it back to society.

62384309
>>62382648
Stupid retard that's not an excuse, warehouse jobs are plenty, factory jobs are plenty
Stupid fucking retard holy shit

62384392
>Unemployment is low
>170k+ jobs created last month
>stocks all in green
Yep, it's demoralarpin' time.

62384401
>>62382655
It’s like the guy whose girlfriend threw his bitcoin hardrive into the trash and he spent 10 years going through the landfill to try and find it. Nigga, your bags are never going to pump. It’s ok to hold them, just move on and do something else with your life. On the off chance they go up, you still have them, but you are too emotionally invested. This is also a reason you will never sell, too much greed and emotions.

62384614
>>62384309
> durrrhurrr stupid reeeetaaaarrrrdddd JUST TAKE A JOB THAT DOESN'T PAY YOU NEARLY ENOUGH TO GET YOUR OWN APARTMENT AND AFFORD FOOD WITHOUT BLEEDING OUT YOUR SAVINGS HURRRRHURRRHURRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR
> NOOOOO BRROOOOOOO YOU'RE SUPPOSED TO START AT THE VERY PITCH BOTTOM AND HAVE MANAGEMENT LIKE YOU ENOUGH TO PROMOTE YOU BROOOOOOOOOO

That aside, this thread has sadly proven biz is long long dead. A few good replies there and there, but most are completely unserious replies like yours.

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 43 replies — 142.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:43 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359931
>>62359749
fuck yourself, im gonna keep snailposting till you retards give me some concrete nostradamus dates and counter reasoning exactly like you retards do on the 1pbtid pepe threads

62359962
Line will continue up until 7/4/28
7*4 = 28 and they get the satisfaction of destroying a holiday. Will also hand everything to the dems in the next election.

62359991
>>62349960
This is now a Flielen thread

62359998
>>62359991

62361834
snailposting for continued feedback, you're not getting rid of me that easily

62361911
>>62349960
Woah wait a second Frieren, the FOMC is next week? Fucccccccckkkk I need to deleverage.
>FOMC -> NDQ goes up
Walk me through this part if they hike wouldn't that cause a dump or something not more pumping? So should I hold my bullish positions or not? Also I'm pretty sure both Anthropic and OpenAI ipo this year in that order.

62361941
>>62353866
this year I saw a lot of ads for oil related jobs masquerading aa youtube shorts
not sure if that counts

62363423
>>62361911
when trump said he'll be signing the agreement on sunday, the target rates dumped

https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/interest-rates/cme-fedwatch-tool.html

warsh is gonna come out next week and be dovish, but the market will act like its not simply because they're range trading, and they're gonna rebuy their positions.

62364277
motheryuckers, this snail post will exist for years

62364284
>>62349960
>metabolized
Stop reading right there.

62366455
>>62364284

don't worry, illiterate retard opinions aren't needed

62366592
>>62366455
I didn't give an opinion.

62368882
snailposting till nostradamus pomp or domp predictions

62370523
snailposting is just transient guys

62370546
>>62370523
Reddit elf

62370557
>>62370546
tard

62370592
>>62370523

How certain are you about that November election? Rs literally gerrymandered the south and killed D seats + added 12. Trump picks have won every primary solidifying his control. I think it will be a narrow rig in order for the country to not go "troubles" Dem or Rep doesn't fucking matter, probably Rep +1 in the house

62372434
>>62370592
hmm good point, but there's no way they can unfuck the public opinion by november. The pain of inflation will be too fresh, and the memory of Iran causing that pain to boot. All the poor people will be out in force

62372555
>>62359780
Is Frieren a virgin?

Thanks for this thread OP

62375431
>>62372555
i am a virgin, yes, i am frieren btw, you're welcome

62378224
i am goy cattle slave

anyway, let's see what warsh has to say tomorrow. I'm guessing it'll be exactly what everyone expects, and the market will dump, because "oh my god it's what we expected therefore we must domp eeet" and then news headlines will make up some bullshit about it not being exactly what everyone expected

then we'll start our final ascent next week

the alternative is as the other anon said and the WC just completely eats any remaining fud and we begin the ascent tomorrow.

idk call it

62378317
>>62349960
I'll play along; assuming you're 100% correct: how do I benefit the most from this timeline?

62378908
>>62378317

For the next leg, just buy a portfolio of ndq & btc, maybe hype too if you're close to arthur and have advanced notice of when their gang is gonna commence_ze_domp.exe. It'd be hard to pick a specific time + stock individually due to how many of them have been bid up already, I think it'll just be ndq as a whole that floats upwards carried by the larger ai stocks getting bid up on anthropic bullish biz announcements


At the top, take out far OTM 3-6 month puts on the stocks way out on the risk curve of AI. You can probably just ask claude or chatgpt to come up with a list, but this list was decent (not great, but ok):

https://www.reddit.com/r/wallstreetbets/comments/1tuwp73/in_the_ai_gold_rush_what_are_the_shovels/

close the puts once spy/ndq are around 15% off the top, nearing the "bear market" 20% fud line where the money printers tend to start spinning

At the 20% fud line, short vix futures


after the initial leg down, you can either assume gold/btc/commodities will do well (like they did in 1973) or continue to be suppressed with the market's decline (like they did in 2000). If they're suppressed, then just scalp spy on each retrace and short it at the top of each retrace (whatever vehicle works here). If they're not suppressed, I'd just buy gld/btc/commodities and capture long term cap gains and chill


just a footnote: the reason I say 3-6 months is because of trump, if he wasn't president, I'd just do 1-2 month. If you feel he won't pull some bullshit, then ya go for it

risk size is whatever you want, don't use this post to construct your reason for suicide pls

62378916
>>62349960
Problem with posts like these is they make the predictions way too fast. Put the 2027 prediction in 2037 and yeah sure.

Remember, "nothing ever happens"

62378940
>>62378317
also, the full retard method for June 22 - Oct 10 assuming im 100% correct would be spcx, but i have no insight to their insiders

62380718
https://www.financialjuice.com/News/9640328/The-US-and-Iran-discuss-moving-up-the-signing-of-the-deal---Axios-citing-sources-.aspx

*bzzzz bzzzzz* "bogdanoff, he panic sold"

"pomp eet"

I guess trump got whatever negotiations with musk and altman in place, or maybe not, or maybe yes, or maybe not, or maybe yes

62381248
https://www.financialjuice.com/News/9640622/Fed-9-of-18-FOMC-participants-pencil-in-a-2026-rate-hike.aspx

*bzzzzt bzzzzzt* "bogdanoff, he bot ze dip"

"domp eet"

>>62378224


> then news headlines will make up some bullshit about it not being exactly what everyone expected

"warsh betrayed us"

62382661
snailpost

62383504
>>62382661
See >>62370546

62384543
>>62383504
kys

anyway, there's not much to add to this thread, and since this board is dead and contributed a grand total of:

- "no, because the world cup"

- "no, because the republicans will win despite losing the war in the most humiliating defeat ever known to humankind"

- random faggots who want to only goon to worthless dogshit threads so they respond with "reddit elf" on actual discussions


there's not much point in staying here


>>62353866

is probably the only meaningful reply

item.title: You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mor… — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mor…

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:57 UTC

OP

You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mortgage. You don't need plane tickets when a road trip can save money. You don't need all those subscriptions. You don't need to hire landscapers. You don't need apple products. You don't need student loans. You don't need a smart fridge. You don't need fancy food for your dog. You don't need a 4k tv. You don't need to go skiing. You don't need a boat. You don't need to eat at restaurants. You don't need a gaming PC. You don't need to pay more than $30 for a haircut. Or anything really. You don't need to shop at Sam's club. You don't need to shop at Costco. You don't need to shop at Best Buy. You don't need to buy a bunch of fancy ingredients and larp as a chef. You don't need a granite counter top. You don't need to buy Claude tokens. You don't need to buy sports tickets. You don't need a cat. You don't need to pay more than $35 for a phone bill. You don't need to pay for new tires. You can get them used. You don't need to buy premium gas. You don't need to tell the insurance company how many people live with you. You don't need to buy any books, use project Gutenberg. You don't need all those tools in the garage. You don't use half of them anyways. You don't need a home with a garage. You don't need to be on the HOA. You don't need to go on weekly dates with your gf. You don't need to go on weekly dates with your wife. You don't need to buy condoms. You don't need to buy diapers. You don't need all sorts of sauces and condiments in the fridge. You don't need warranties ANY warranty, just take care of your things. You don't need to go to concerts. You don't need to have a blue checkmark. You don't need to eat food at events. You don't need a house with stairs. You don't need carpet on those stairs. You don't need a leather couch. You don't need to have the fastest wifi. You don't need to buy pornographic art. You don't need to buy beer. Or any drug.

Early replies (first 3)

62377958
>>62377955
man no one is reading allat nigga fucking die in a fire.

62378430
some wealthy people have so much money they don't even have enough time to spend it. the costs of things is just another thing that takes time. the secret is to remove the things that gatekeep time so the time can be spent on things you want not have to

62378534
>>62377955
I don't know what you meant by this but this was the most relaxing thing I've ever read and it describes my life and outlook

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378563
>>62377955
>You don't need to buy condoms
Smile crept onto my face. Based

62378597
>>62377955
you don't need to live kys faggot

62378610
>>62377958
this.
only thing people need to read is the fact I need 10k.

62378617
>>62377955
I'm not reading that wall of text.

I already have a new car (Toyota Corolla) paid off. My rent is $900/mo and I split it with my mom, so it's really only $450. I don't like sitting in a car for hours or driving on a freeway. A flight to Miami from where I live is about 2.5 hours, a car ride would be 19 hours if you don't stop for food, restroom, gas or sleep.

62378622
>>62378617
no one asked

62378624
>>62377955
I do want some of these things however, and I will use money to buy them. What, am I supposed to live miserably like some FIRE redditor and spend the best years of my life physically on wage slaving for a future that may never come or that I will be too damaged to enjoy? No thanks.

62378680
>>62378622
ESL?

Learn proper grammar, retard.

62378689
>>62378680
you might want to go back to school if you think that this isn't proper grammar, nigger

62378695
>>62377955
I need the cute and funny tho.

62378857
based op

62378869
>>62378689
>He still doesn't capitalize the first letter, or end a comment with a period.

62378877
>>62378869
this has nothing to do with proper grammar or ESL. Nobody types like this any longer, fucking boomer

62378880
>>62378877
Don't text me until you're educated enough to have an intellectual conversation.

62378883
>>62378880
take the L, grandpa

62378886
>>62378883
*Accept you have lost, senior citizen.

62378888
>>62378886
bro, just say you have asperger, you already gave it away the moment you said you live with your mom

62378897
>>62378888
Quads don't lie...

62378926
>>62377958
why do you speak like a little nigger from the jungle ?

62379963
>>62377955
tell that to the communist invaders who are actually materialists

62379983
>>62377955
>You don't need to shop at Sam's club. You don't need to shop at Costco.
but those places save you money...

62380058
yeah, and commies should get shot at the head and get hanged on electric poles with their guts out

62380088
>>62377955
I need all of that + more, so fuck you and fork it over porky *shoves gun in your face*

62380106
>>62377955
>You don't need to pay more than $35 for a phone bill
>$35
thats way too much. 700pc overpriced. mint mobile is $5/mo.
get on my level.

62380264
>>62380106
>Mint mobile - $15/mo
Get on my level, Tello mobile.
$10/ account .
I already rolled over 3 numbers there that I don't need. And have 1 extra backup phone and phone line.

62382946
>>62380264
>>62380106
ENTER
https://www.ebay.com/itm/136840233242

62382962
>>62377955
You are right OP. Every single company providing these goods and services should go broke from loss of revenue. All wagies need to lose their jobs.

62382977
>>62377955
I need my 2k a month mortgage or i will lose my house. Also, i'm not getting rid of my cats.

62383138
Also, i'm not getting rid of my cats

62384604
>>62377955
Costco is excellent THO

62384613
Also Sam's Club saves you money in the long run, buying bulk is the way of the future .

item.title: How is your career going? — 61 replies — 128.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is your career going?

Open thread • Replies: 61 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:33 UTC

OP

I make $28/hr as a Fire Alarm Inspection Technician. I have 2 licenses and 4.5 years of experience:
-FASA/BASA
-NICET Inspection and Testing Level 2

Early replies (first 3)

62381055
Going for the NICET Fire Alarm Systems Level 1 license in the next 2 months or so. Will hopefully get to $30/hr.

62381060
>>62381049
*chirp*

62381078
>>62381049
is that a fun job? sounds comfy desu

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382789
>>62382786
Writing code is extremely boring with Claude anyway, and refusing to use it is not an option

62382806
35 an hour doing monkey data entry (accounts payable) at a hospital. I have a bachelors in accounting and this is my first entry level job. Before this I worked at an Amazon warehouse. Took me a whole 5 years to get an office job. I barely tried though so it’s my fault
t. 28

62382818
>>62381049
I've been stuck in public service for 12 years doing work I hate. I recommended my coworker who turned out to be a complete fucking idiot once he started working here. Hes going to get us both fired. I have missed out on better opportunities here because I am too nice and didn't advocate for myself. Its all so tiresome.

62382822
>>62381049
Have you identified which government regulations secure your job?
Seems like an important thing to know.

62382877
>>62381049
27m, 150k, fully remote. on track to become senior and make ~180k

swe

62382899
>>62381049
24m
$45/hr as a Information Management/Technical Consultant.
Been at it for 4 years:
-Mostly just showing people how to organize data and operate basic proprietary corpo programs.
-90% of the work is physical (walking in, seeing something has obviously been done wrong, fixing it)
Job security is that people will always have trouble with the machines they use on a day to day basis. People will always be stupid and mess things up.

>>62382184
On the equipment side, stenotype's have been around since the 1910's so you can easily get comfortable with the layout on a typewriter from the 1950's for $30.

>>62382303
>Weekly hours?
It's rural, my usual is roughly 30 hours. I get calls from 100+ miles away who are willing to pay enough to have me come out and teach.
A lot of the extra dough comes from just claiming what the company wants to through out tech wise, then wiping and reselling their entire server (made in 2021) online or in person through third party marketplaces. Make about double what the hourly is with that.

>>62382530
>how did you get started
I joke to a lot of the upper class clients "no one hired me, so I hired myself"

>>62382877
Is it niche work? How do you prevent your job from getting jeeted out?

62382947
>>62382899
>Is it niche work? How do you prevent your job from getting jeeted out?

we have some foreign contractors but we have a shit ton of funding covering burn so we can afford to hire citizens. it's not a unique business but we're niche in our approach and field (data/ai). we're actually hiring right now.

62383011
33m 110k a year working in crypto, remote.
Workplace changed from based and nice to kinda idiotic thanks to poor business decisions, AI brain rot, and limited runway.

I'm trying to change but everything else pays like shit or is a cult. IT makes me want to kill myself at this point. I have golden handcuffs

I just want to find a way to accumulate so much capital that I can live off dividends.

62383016
32k CAD ($20.25/hr) after being at WMT for 10 years
I know it's not the best job, but it's low effort, my coworkers and bosses and customers love me, and I can keep working sustainably. I've managed to get about 140k CAD net worth, too.

62383085
>>62382530
Basically I got hired from a craigslist posting for a middleman tech company ran by some bulgarian. He would send us out to clients and pay us less than half, scalping the rest. I have a legitimate interest in tech and build things with hardware, networks and software so some of these places I helped set up and was friendly helpful to them. It also let me learn the missing parts of each field and then I was able to piece it all together. Since they were the clients, I would interact with the CEOs, Owners and top end leadership of each company. You vibe well with them and not be inappropriate (hitting on, trying to get numbers, be a creep) they will want you, even do power plays to get you. I quit the middleman cause he would send me to do shit that gets paid 170k salary (85/hr) and pay me 21/hr and scalp the other 64, while not helping me really. I was able to chill for a year, then i reached out to one of these clients and they picked me right up. Since they wanted me, I had the leverage to negotiate a higher salary, which they had no problem paying. They liked me so much they just have me run the whole IT department, I dont even really have oversight. I do have to know my shit, but AI makes it 1000x easier and if you arent using AI you arent gonna make it. Literally complete tasks that would take months to do in days. These guys can't even fathom that kind of efficiency so they think im busy all the time. AI is literally a cheat code, especially if you know your shit and use it to do that shit. I'm close to 6 figs starting, will easily hit 6 figs after solve some of their money loss headaches (which i have done for a major part).
>Getting interest from potential employers gives you huge leverage, esp if they know you are a game changer
The best part is I asked if I could start my own projects for learning, that they would benefit the company and the CEO is hyped for it. I am literally getting paid to self improve

62383096
>>62381049
I'm a warehouse grunt
$20 / hour
How do I even go up from here? Maybe a forklift license?

62383304
I make 31 an hour doing quality assurance for asphalt and I think about killing myself every day.

62383355
>>62381049
I make $29/hour driving the city bus.
I have a class B CDL.
While the pay is nothing special, the benefits are incredible, the hours are great, and the company overall is a dream to work for.
I plan on leeching every last cent I can out of this place before getting a fat retirement in 30 years.

62383464
>>62382153
It won't, everything has yearly inspections

62383470
Oh yeah its going fucking awful. I make $23/hr annoying people at their homes. I fully expect to be killed one day doing this but someone's gotta do it

62383481
$48 per hour? Thats $2000 per week. You can buy otm SPY contracts and double your weekly net. The issue is. Nobody want to work learn a life changing skill outside of their job because theyre so mentally exhausted from everyday distraction & life. We call ourselves the 1%'ers

62383500
>>62381049
late 20's, 120K in business analytics. It's an adult daycare but the pay's good, feels bad going in the office once a week tho. There are some qts around but that's just about all my social interaction for the week. My dream is to keep the same amount of pay but work in another country in another time zone, but that would be rough. Seriously thinking about it one day

62383513
i make $130k base + 15% bonus target as a corporate accounting manager. i'm underpaid by like $20k/yr but i'm fully remote so i accept it. kind of wish i had done law school instead.

62383520
>>62381049
Just bombed a senior product manager and coordinator interview.
I talk too much and don’t get to the point. That’s the direct feedback I got, which is true.

I have another offer I already accepted at a faang company though. It’s lower level and need to move though.

62383530
>>62383520
>It’s lower level and need to move though.
are you going to do it? i can't imagine moving for work in the year 2026, when these big indianjewnigger companies can (and do) blindly lay people off all the time.

62383536
>>62381049
100% stuck at a job I hate that boring as fuck most days and the few days where I actually need to work I bust my ass off all day because my retarded managers will fuck the schedule up and have only one person on to do three peoples worth of work. I lucked into a middle management position though in a field with basically no mobility so I can't really leave without taking a huge paycut. Make $36/hr which feels like nothing because I also live in a pretty expensive area.

62383538
>>62383096
Whatever you want anon. Start a business on the side or smthg.
>>62383530
I will because can’t take the current company anymore or I will Elliott Rodger someone in Minecraft.

62383574
>>62382822
Commerical and residential buildings in my state literally cannot be occupied if they don't pass a fire inspection

62383585
I deliver mail for 30 bucks an hour. Paid for 8.5 hours usually done in 5-6, it's a weird evaluated quazi salaried thing.

62384021
>>62381049
Early 40s, trapped in a flyover shithole making $80k in data analytics. Entry level title with a senior-level workload and enough experience to at least land interviews for data engineering roles with some regularity, though there are only a handful of openings in my area each year. I'm pretty frugal and on track to be able to semi-retire in Asia within a few years which is looking increasingly likely given the state of the job market and my career.

62384045
i'm a family doc and make ~130/hr. it sucks but at least my schedule is condensed into 4 days a week and i'm hoping to retire in 9 years.

62384057
>42
>middle manager but my job is going to be outsourced in the next year or so
>110k before taxes
>live in New York suburbs so it’s actually not as much money
I have a lot invested and saved so when I’m axed it’s tempting to just try to survive on that instead of working again. no family to support and I inherited the house I live in with no mortgage.

62384184
>$37/hour salary equivalent post taxes and $1k a month 401k contribution
is this good

62384198
>>62384184
Depends on CoL in your area, but it's better than most.

62384480
$50k cad customer support
36 yrs old
I am severely underperforming but I love making music -- married a woman from a well off family, we have a house
Confidence low, bros, confidence low

item.title: What happened to META? — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What happened to META?

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:58 UTC

OP

What happened to META?

Early replies (first 3)

62375138
>>62375027
What happened to Metaverse?

62375415
they completely whiffed on AI

62375425
2010s business model in 2020s

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378143
>>62375027
that crypto company? digial land isn't real nor scarce

oh wait you mean FACEBOOK that boomer company that old people use to post their goy opinions on?

LMAO

62378194
saaaaaaaaar

62378208
Despite their desperate clutching at straws they are struggling to stay relevant. Even MS which flopped absolutely everything they tried in the last 25 years is still on.

62378228
>>62375027
irrelevant company

62378234
>>62375027
It made too much profit. Top stocks can't make huge profits. There value needs to be hopium based.

62378483
>>62375027
literally nothing going for them anymore. metaverse failed. their AI models are garbage. their core products are tepid growth at best and more places are banning social media. only hope left is elon buying their data centers and gutting the rest.

62378502
>>62375138
>>62375415
/thread

Turns out you can't actually run a company by getting lucky with one idea (that was stolen)

62380977
>>62378483
their vr play is still king

62382772
>>62380977
I literally tried to play GOLF+ VR with my friends on my Meta Quest 2 earlier and it took nearly half an hour just to load. Meta OS recently had a shitty major release that is so bad that it disabled the in-game music feature. You used to be able to listen to youtube podcasts in GOLF+ and that doesn't even work anymore because Meta broke the integration

62382776
>>62382772
You ever gooned in it?

62382836
>>62375138
They goyim dealt in it

62382889
>>62375027
repeated failed projects like >>62375138
all their successes now are platforms they just bought like instagram
they do more than people realize but they can't be ranked next to apple and google as sure fire bets anymore imo

62383358
>>62375415
They are doing a lot of AI stuff especially in the open source sector.

62383373
>>62378143
retard, META owns Instagram, Facebook and Whatsapp. You're a retarded incel if you think they're niche normie platforms

62384014
>>62382772
>Meta Quest
I'm talking about Meta Quest 3 512GB

62384081
The worst management ever happened. I have a friend working there, and his colleagues are currently sabotaging the work since the company is trying to train AI on them lol

62384088
>>62375138
That's not funny motherfucker

62384122
>>62383358
problem is it doesn't fucking work
meta's AI products are completely terrible
this is compounded by the fact that they also paid higher salaries than anyone for AI talent
zuckerberg literally paid top dollar for dogshit at the hundreds of billions level

62384142
>>62375027
both facebook and instagram are horrible websites to use, they just exist because of the already big user base, but they will continue to slowly decline

62384618
Zucc spent all the money doubling down on metaverse and then had to go into debt to hire a stylist/pr firm in a pathtic attempt to rebrand his image

item.title: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker makes new crypto tax law even for personal wallets. — 23 replies — 72.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Illinois Governor JB Pritzker makes new crypto tax law even for personal wallets.

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:42 UTC

OP

Illinois Governor JB Pritzker makes new crypto tax law even for personal wallets. What was meant by this?

Early replies (first 3)

62380022
>>62380012
Imagine paying this kek

62380027
>>62380022
I'm from Chicago, I don't have to

62380078
>>62380012
Corrupt billionaire piece of shit doesn't like it when normal people try to get ahead. He gets the rope too
Has to be a thick steel rope though or this fat fuck would snap it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62380133
>>62380012
what a fat retard

62380143
>>62380027
Is Chicago like India of USA?

62380182
poor people are NOT allowed to escape taxes

62380274
Fuck this fat commie retard, nobody should pay this kek. Leftists think theyre aligned against the billionaires or some shit but they sure do like electing them

62380289
>>62380012
>What was meant by this?
>new tax on crypto in Chicago / Illinois
He means that Illionois and Chicago is going bankrupt.
And the Democrats need to tax you.
If you already didn't know that democrats ALWAYS increase taxes on you, I can't help you.

62380292
>>62380012
He’s still trying to make ends meet after the city sold its parking meter revenue to Saudi Arabia

62381177
>>62380012
That he is jewish.

62381218
>>62380012
Only true fucking retards still live in illinois.

62381394
>>62380143
Think India but frozen instead of sweltering.

62382073
this is the dumbest things i have ever heard right after the unrealized gains tax in the netherlands

62382233
>>62380012
unenforceable bullshit

62382240
>>62380012
does this fat fuck EVER stop eating lmao

62382364
>>62380012
Good. People should be investing in productive enterprises like Blackstone instead of monopoly money for incels.

62382391
Imagine voluntarily living within the geospatial land boundary known as Niggernois in the big '26

62382397
>>62382240
his cock doesnt work anymore so no. all he has is food and petty tyranny.

62382528
>>62380143
no that is Texas now, stupid fucking wheely mcsped went on a whole bendover for them not too long ago promoting h1b's and how the US needed to import jeets instead of spics

62382563
>>62382528
Texas has been retarded for years. They decided to try and hook companies in by offering 0% business taxes, thinking they'd hire natives. Instead they imported millions of miserable californians. Lmao

62383767
>>62380012
>reorganize personal wallets
>get taxed for it
And exactly how does this get enforced?

62383771
*boom boom crash*
YOU DIDNT PAY YOUR GOODGOY DIGITAL TAX FOR GREATER ISRAEL
ON THE FLOOR NOW CHUM

62384535
>>62383767
It just means you have to clean every. single. bitcoin through Monero to evade it because Bitcoin transactions are public. They'll look through the ledger and go "OY, THESE WOILLETS HAD THESE TRANSACTIONS WHERE'S MY CUT" and that's it. They can do anything they want as long as they have the manpower to arrest you for it. They can charge you on imaginary taxes or taxes on unrealized gains, which is only a step beyond taxing you on wallet transfers.

item.title: TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:56 UTC

OP

TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN

Early replies (first 3)

62381997
>>62381962
Can't he just take the dividend and up it to like 20%? That should drive the price back up because people like free money right? What's stopping him?

62382004
>>62381997
not going to help if he's not going to maintain peg

62382061
To bad Saylor can't release a stable coin to pump BTC that would require DeFi lol. Tom Lee however will mention this in the next shareholder meeting.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382284
>>62382061
btc to 7k
shtlink 50.....cent - HAHAHAHA

62382319
>>62381962
the bottom is unironically $0

62382323
>>62382250
Yeah, 32 years if he sells bitcoin, but I bet that doesn't add up to the panic he will cause by a massive sell off, also liquidity, I doubt any market is ready for this retard to dump 32billion in bitcoin.

Btw, he has maybe 7-8 months of cash left to pay for dividends if he doesn't sell bitcoin or doesn't dillute his shares.

I think we can all see where this is heading to

62382325
>>62382323
54* billion

62382328
>>62382323
>7-8 months
bear market will be over by then

62382337
>>62382328
>the bear market will be over despite the biggest holder being underwater and forced to sell soon
yeah sure anon i can just FEEL the bull fomo

62382345
>>62381962
and I don't think anyone ever commented this here properly, but the impact in other protocals which relies mostly of their liquidity pool in bitcoin will be huge, imagine once people would start selling off their bitcoin or trading to any stablecoin, that will depeg as well. We had just a briefly taste of that in Luna, some other protocols that relied in Wrapped Luna in their holdings, I recall USDT, USDC depegging for a brief time and a random protocol depegging pretty bad.

I don't think people realize how in the shit this market will be if he fucks up hard.

62382352
>>62382328
I don't know, we are heading to 2 rate hikes this year and a bubble worse than .dot bursting at the same time. I don't feel comfortable at all to say that this 4 year cycle will keep untouched.

62382359
>>62382352
dotcom*

62384528
>>62382345
Usdt and usdc are backed by fixed income instruments, not saylors money magic, which like UST, is basically a derivative of Bitcoin/MSTR

62384592
>>62381997
The dividend IS almost 20% at that price lmao.

62384600
>>62381997
And pay the dividends with what money? He would have to offload a shitton of BTC. That's the crux of the issue

62384605
WERE SO BACK

item.title: I am most ashamed that I was too stupid to see how this Chainlink shit would end; or that I didn’t wait till I better un… — 31 replies — 52.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am most ashamed that I was too stupid to see how this Chainlink shit would end; or that I didn’t wait till I better un…

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:26 UTC

OP

I am most ashamed that I was too stupid to see how this Chainlink shit would end; or that I didn’t wait till I better understood the situation here in the higher levels of the chains leadership before I sold $LINK as a dream to my friends, my family, and even mine and my friends companies.

Personally did harm to my friends by selling them this shit.

Definitely learned to never trust narratives, dig deeper than face value and better test the tech before promoting it; for anything moving forward.

I was absolutely in the cult of Sergey; only difference between me and most is I was blocked by Chainlink Labs for disagreeing with narratives before I knew the full picture (circa 2026)

Early replies (first 3)

62377672
>>62377660
Your friends are dumb as hell for trusting a retard. Chainlink your balls to my face.

62377692
>>62377660
At least you learned something out of all this OP, also never push investment choices on anyone else just in case it does blow up and you look like a retard. People will always brush off and forget proportional to medium size wins but will never forget a loss. It's happened to me once, never again.

62380598
>>62377660
Cucklink = Russian ponzi scheme

Sergey is the only person who /madeit/ with Chainlink

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62381301
>>62377660
idk anon we haven't even really started the game yet and you're calling the outcome? LINK lives or dies by its tradfi adoption. 2026 will be the first year we see some of that come online and it'll be years before we see the real adoption curve play out.

62381444
>>62381301
>Just 2 more weeks bro

Is what a fuddie would like to post, unwilling to provide a real retort to this argument

62381930
>>62381444
Trips, Kek really wants me to sell (nice try Kek)

62381939
>>62381301
>2018 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2019 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2020 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2021 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2022 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2023 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2024 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2025 will be the first year we see some of that come online

62382102
>>62381939
>2026 chainlink turned me gay

62382292
please don't sell, ok? please

62382347
these disingenuous larps and appeals are very gross
if any of it is true then you have no one to blame but yourself, retard, and whining on a mongolian sheep breeding bulletin board while addressing nothing of substance beyond ridiculous claims of causing harm by proffering financial advice is peak faggotry
but who am I kidding, it's the same band of mentally ill freak faggots it always is

62383388
>>62382347
>disingenuous
you overuse this word to the point it's clear you're projecting

62383866
>>62377660

thank you for posting this. if you wouldn't mind dropping by the coinfessions thread in the catalog and leaving a note that would help some other cult members see the light


your friends will forgive you, just be honest with them

62383878
>>62383388
>you overuse this word to the point it's clear you're projecting


spot on. you can tell he's having the same thoughts as op but just can't quite bring himself to confront the realization he was grifted. totally understandable as it is a hit to ego. he might just not be ready yet

62383923
>>62381444
>>62381939
unless you're a newfag you've known the entire time we're waiting for SWIFT and DTCC to go online for the real fireworks to begin. I'm not saying anything is guaranteed but quitting at the starting line after waiting years for the show seems kind of crazy to me. Do whatever you need, though. I'm not even a dr;ns fag, I'm definitely selling one day, but literally nothing has happened for 9 years and now all of a sudden we have hard evidence that this year something is going to happen, so I am at least going to see if that affects the price positively or not.

62383986
>>62383388
>>62383878
are you fags trolling? I used the word once.
>baited

62384008
Sergey delivered on almost everything he promised. He never promised that the token would skyrocket in value. Your failure was assuming that the tokens value was connected to ChainLink Lab's success as a company

62384026
>>62384008
>Your failure was assuming that the tokens value was connected to ChainLink Lab's success as a company
Link is involved every step of the way for anything Chainlink offers.
The more Chainlink is used the greater the demand for Link.

And so far not one crypto has seen enough usage to pump based on that alone, it's all been almost exclusively speculative.

62384049
>>62384026
LINK powers almost the entirety of the DeFi space already and it has meant jack shit for the price. CLL has been making direct deals with DeFi companies and as a little carrot to token holders they decided to create the strategic reserve and use some of the funds to buy LINK to create some artificial demand for the token.

62384064
>>62384049
see >>62384026
>so far not one crypto has seen enough usage to pump based on that alone, it's all been almost exclusively speculative.

It's almost like you can't read or something.

62384067
>>62384064
>$100 billion isn't enough
Keep coping

62384103
>>62384067
Not even ETH at its highest demand for gas has pumped based on Defi usage.

In fact, ETH has nearly three times higher demand for gas today than it did in late 2021, but the fiat price is over three times lower.

62384120
>>62384103
demand for eth plummeted because gas fees went from like $20 to $1 during peak bullruns

62384125
>>62384120
>demand for eth plummeted
demand for ETH is at an absolute ATH.

It's 3x higher than at ETH's fiat ATH; and 200x higher than at ETH's sats ATH.

62384181
>>62384049
DeFi is a microcosm of the financial world, it's pennies compared to a vault. Most of the DeFi activity is fake. Zero tokens currently hold any real fundamental foundation (e.g. usage alone is pumping price anywhere meaningful). ETH could literally be a couple bucks or less and still function just fine at the volume it is today. The entire market is speculative, but the speculative fervor stages are now behind us. There are no more men riding caravans to the mountains to dig for gold, now the bankers and railroadmen are setting up and building a town and buying the land the gold sits beneath. This next phase is everything for crypto. Linkies are betting LINK wins from that phase, non-linkies are betting on I have no idea what.. some scam from yesteryear to get another death spiral pump n dump. BTC, ETH are probably ok, maybe SOL and a couple others, but none stand to benefit like LINK does. I can't even respect anyone who's in crypto right now and doesn't hold at least some LINK. 99% of crypto is about to permadie.

62384194
>>62384049
>CLL has been making direct deals with DeFi companies
Who keeps paying you to spam this nonsense all day every single day, by the way?

62384273
>what is Layer 2 scaling
>what are gas dynamics
>how does EIP-1559 work and how does Layer 2 affect it
>the paid shiller cannot distinguish between computational capacity and dollar amount spent to buy ETH
>total ETH Fee Demand=Gas Used×Gas Price
you just KNOW it's over when the paid shiller uses such stupid arguments in order to draw the most fringe and pathetic comparison to their own ERC shitcoin (which btw REQUIRES ETH in order to function)
ETH is dead, the entire market is dead, no adoption is coming, you can keep focusing on the paid pilots and marketing blogposts all you want, but the reality is you're an absolute moron
>but muh DTCC said this and that!
the very fact that DTCC "integrated" blatant scams such as Layer Zero and Stellar should tell you that they don't give a flying fuck, but when you spend 10 years shilling and holding a particular shitcoin, to the point that you adopt an entire online personality around that particular shitcoin, you cease to be objective, and you have a vested interest to sell your bags to others
it's over guys, the sooner you realize this the better

62384299
>>62384273
>what is

It's all shit anon. It doesn't matter at all

62384339
>>62384273
triggered so hard he had to switch IDs again kek

>it's over guys
That's why you're here passionately crying your eyes out right?

62384367
>triggered so hard he couldn't restrain himself from yet another shitpost that doesn't address a single argument and resorts to ad homs
embarrassing, also this was my first and only post ITT, if this is the best LINK shills can come up with, of course it's over
it's been over for a while now tho, but the sheer desperation and panic of shills lately is the final nail in the coffin for LINK
>pls pls pls keep holding bros, le DTCC and SWIFT are going online NAO!

62384383
>>62384367
"It's over" but you're here getting argumentative like a chick on PMS, and absolutely rattled in the process.
Tell us, what bags are you holding? We're all dying to know Mr. market understander.

62384428
>the shill with the most pbtid who desperately tries to convince anons to buy/keep holding resorts to endless projection and ad homs
poetic

item.title: NO NO NO NOT AGAIN AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO NOT AGAIN AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-18 16:01 UTC

OP

NO NO NO NOT AGAIN AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH

Early replies (first 3)

62383370
unc crashout

62383372
again and again and again and again and again and again and again

62383669
its dumping massive and the weekend hasnt even started !!!!
btc at 57k on monday

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383712
Why is it happening? I thought Iran war ending would be bullish.

Is it cause the new fed chair indicating they would stop the forward signaling or whatever the fuck

62383790
>>62383669
Possible but doubtful. The market usually takes about 3 days tops to digest a fed meeting and by the looks of things the majority of the move has already been priced in

62383921
>>62383712
>Why is it happening? I thought Iran war ending would be bullish.

Reminder that us Bobos thought the start of the war would be bearish back in march

62383963
>>62383261
I think i get why boomers tell u to just throw it in a etf and forget

these same boomers probably went through this hell when they were our age getting raped in the markets.

62384178
Let the goyim deal in the real world

62384216

62384228

item.title: Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor — 50 replies — 34.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-18 14:35 UTC

OP

Hello /biz/ I will try and keep this brief
Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO as I only just recently started investing
It seemed simple, solid mid-long term option, and sensical for starting out to not fuck up while getting exposure
Now, this has nothing to do with FOMO, but I’m wondering if I should start aiming towards investing in either specific sector ETFs or maybe specific stocks to chase higher returns
My portfolio is currently at $60K mostly in VOO, perhaps with enough DCA I can reach $100k by years end or at least close to that, depends on growth
Currently my total portfolio return ranges from 17-20% depending on the month for context
I’ve seen recently how well things like SOXX have been doing and was considering finding a sector ETF or two to go into since I already have so much broad market exposure
To be clear, this is not me chasing returns based off past performance, I am well aware growth has to come from somewhere and is not infinite so I’m considering CAGRs as far back as possible
With all this in mind, any recommendations? Find a good sector ETF, go into a specific stock, or maybe just focus on getting more income first and then continue to just VOO and chill
And no I don’t wanna sell options doing covered calls or buy your shit coin etc etc
I should add the sectors I’m interested in are semi-conductors, energy (ones that deal with technology needed to cool data centers), and perhaps drones / nuclear for commercial and military usage

Early replies (first 3)

62369867
>>62369835
XLK
SOXL

62369880
>>62369835
Recreate the ETF so you avoid paying management fees.. Not super relevant for VOO, but if you go into niche sector specific ETFs, the management fees are usually over 0.5%

62370000
>>62369867
Not sure how I feel about the triple leverage with SOXL can you red pill me on it, it’s the same deal as QQQ vs TQQQ or whatever the hell it’s called right
But otherwise you’re just advising do some tech and semiconductor either way
>>62369880
Fair point I’ll consider that

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62371396
>>62369835
Basically I want to be your boyfriend or just glorified cockcleaner while both of our ports build up to 100k (but mine faster than yours but that's okay)

62371737
Also my portfolio currently sits at $61,000
I have a goal of reaching 100k by year end
There’s 174 week days left in the year, ignoring market closed due to holiday
100,000 - 61,000 = 39,000
39,000 / 174 = 224
224 / 61,000 = .003
So
If my math is right
My account only needs to grow .3% per market day to reach 100,000
61,000 * 1.003^174 = 102,728
Am I correct here

62371763
>>62371385
>It seems like this AI shit isn’t going away any time
It's not, this shit is going to fundamentally change most industries in a way that most people cannot even understand. Its primary limitation is just that older generations can't adapt to it, which means it has naysayers now and a clear path of progressive growth over time, similar to computing decades ago.

It's not just AI though honestly. These companies are the future. With population growth leveling out worldwide, there's not going to be a sudden expansion of demand for food, for minerals, for many traditional industries. NASDAQ is better primed for investment in future tech, hence why I prefer it. Or tech ETFs like VGT, but those are less diversified. Just sinking 50/50 into SP500 and NASDAQ might be fine for you to let it ride

62371848
>>62370048
SEX

62372037
>>62371763
i see, thanks for the input

62372074
>>62371737
Heyyy we're port buddies :) YAGMI

62372118
>>62371737
also this fails to recognize buying more shares bringing that rate down significantly

62372136
>>62372074
>>62371737
I lied my port's now at 77k LOL YANGMI

62372167
Due to volatility and human nature its actually mentally easier to go into 5 companies you believe in. keep most money in S&P and add 5

62372242
>>62372136
errrrm ok buddy

62373602
Do we got a chance for 100k by years end fellas

62373605
>hey guys daddy got me a job making 6 figs, please tell me how to be a millionaire
Nepobabies really think life is just people giving them all the tips to succeed, for free, huh. Lol.

62373753
>>62373602
Im all in on nasdaq but yes

62374176
mmmm sweaty office boobies mmm yes
asphyxiated to death by her big milkies mmmm
yes

62374196
>>62370048
>I miss the touch of a girl
shut the frick up fakecel

62374412
>>62373753
What in particular are you in

62374944
>>62373602
Tbh I want to raise the amount of principal I’m putting into the account but have zero ideas for side money to put into it

62377984
>>62374944
bumpin this idea

62377988
>>62370048
Love is gay.

62377998
>>62369835
Triples and I will chop my cock off

62378015
>>62377998
holy fuck

62378019
>>62374412
NDQ on the asx

62378395
>>62377998
Circumcise it , you got close enough

62379025
>>62378356
errrrrm middle class bro?

62379549
Going over this one last time, Nasdaq has been eating S&P’s lunch, but only because mega cap tech stocks have had a great decade
Additionally, it isn’t that VOO has had bad returns, since it crosses over into a lot of QQQM, just that QQQM is more volatile
So basically it’s a choice on whether you believe the companies doing good will continue to do good with the current market and AI trends, and how volatile you’re allowing your portfolio to be

62379580
>>62371737
Brother, I’m just an anon on this dead basket weaving forum, but this isn’t 1970 or 2010 or even 2020. Get the fuck out of VOO.

62379597
>>62379580
>what do you suggest then?
Physical gold/silver, guns, bullets, tools, food with long shelf life, eth/link in September

62379618
>>62379580
>>62379597
This is the equivalent of Ketotards on /fit/

62382033
>>62379618
lol

62384221
>>62369835
Bumping cause I’m still unsure if I pair VOO with QQQ and just have a ton of overlap and be weighted towards market cap or just do a sector ETF like SOXX or just all VOO (all QQQ seems dumb to me)

item.title: Uh? This will have consequences — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Uh? This will have consequences

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:57 UTC

OP

https://www.usdebtclock.org/

Are you all just shit coin investors? Did any of you even bother to do the math on this?

Literally, the US is going to enter into debt insolvency at about 55 trillion. That will happen pretty fast.

What does this have to do with business?

Well, the US has lost HALF of it's money's value in the last 25 years. That is going to get exponentially worse.

I get it, twenty years ago, people reeeee'd about "meh hyper inflations"... but seriously, the hour glass is losing sand fast.
|
The public debt will become your problem eventually.

Early replies (first 3)

62384457
wrong

62384460
>>62384445
>This will have consequences
cope

62384462
wen doe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384477
>>62384445
Two weeks
I was being lectured on ThE GrEat CrAsH when I was a kid and still nothing

62384542
>>62384477
underage b&

62384570
>>62384445
>big numbers I don't understand are scary!
the world was supposed to end in 2020 and 2012 too right?

62384584
>>62384460

>because it just wont ok
kek lmao even

62384611
>>62384460
Retard. I bet if you asked the Roman Empire back then if they thought they would ever collapse they would probably think you are insane. Prepare now.

item.title: I made a stupidly autistic workbook to track and project all of my income and expenses and account balances. — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I made a stupidly autistic workbook to track and project all of my income and expenses and account balances.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-18 15:57 UTC

OP

I made a stupidly autistic workbook to track and project all of my income and expenses and account balances. i have nobody else to show it to. enjoy.

the payroll calculation sheet includes the full IRS steps that my company's payroll system uses to calculate withholding and everything so i can predict nearly to the penny what my take-home pay will be given a certain number of hours worked, etc

the 401k and home equity numbers don't take growth into account, they're just starting with a recent balance and adding principal deposits only

this has had the neat effect of showing me exactly how grievous an error it is not to max out my 401k contribution up to my employer's match, because i can directly see how much net cash i'm leaving on the table (especially summed at year-end) by doing so, even if i have less liquid cash

Early replies (first 3)

62384424
So i actually like it. Shame you dont have a blank template to share with everyone to try out.

62384465
>>62384414
for predicting utility bills, i have logged all of my utility bills since we got this house in 2022 so i can predict with decent accuracy how much my energy or water bill will be for a decent month

i took all of my planned, regular transactions across all of my accounts, including all of the quarterly and yearly bills like annual insurance premiums and kids' summer camp registrations and blah blah blah and created repeating transaction series just extending outward into future dates.

so for utilities, i have planned transactions for each month that take the monthly average energy/water and then add like 30% margin and I'm always covered and usually have some gravy left over.

for day-to-day household spending, i just transfer my wife 1700 for all groceries and goods for the month (family of 5 + dog, that number is for all household goods including paper towels, detergent, baby diapers, dish soap, all that shit adds up so fast), and then put a lump sum in our joint account for 'family discretionary' expenses and gas for the cars. family discretionary is, obviously, discretionary shit like haircuts, going to the zoo, clothes for us or the kids, gifts for other people, the rare chick-fil-a trip. i don't track/project the nitty gritty there, it's just a lump allocation every month.

62384502
>>62384424
it'd take me a bit to strip my shit out, may do so if the thread is still up later. there's nothing groundbreaking here though, it's just very personalized to my favorite excel methods (tables and structured references and so forth).

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384615
i can predict exactly how much nitpicking this screenshot will invite, but doing THIS was a colossal aid in planning, all of this shit that's non-monthly or on weird intervals and is generally hard to properly plan for. I laid it all out and then sum up the annual total, split that into a per-month set-aside (rounded up) that i auto-transfer into a separate checking account, and now this stuff never comes as a surprise.

making the transaction log was the natural consequence, where I projected these out onto future dates and plotted what the account balance will be, so i can see if i need to shore it up so it won't go negative in six months or whatever.

item.title: Canadian Mortgages are insane — 17 replies — 15.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Canadian Mortgages are insane

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-18 14:53 UTC

OP

Anyone that bought homes over the last 10 years has no concept of the magnitude of the level of debt they have.

Imagine 400k to 900k mortgages on cookie cutter homes with no space between them. This is just fine in the mind of the average leaf.

Let me be clear, they are never going to pay it off. Never. They think because everyone else has a big ass mortgage that its fine. It took me 17 years just to pay down my 228k mortgage to 68k where it is now. How the fuck do these people think at 38 with a 500k mortgage they can pay it down by the time they retire? LMAO.

Fucking totally disconnected from reality. Millions of us.

Early replies (first 3)

62382768
im sick of this being a thing, just fucking crash already. I have to deal with crashes 100 times a year as a crypto cuck but everyone else in the world with their houses and their gay stocks is just driving off into the sunset. make it make sense nigger

62382788
>>62382765
their retirement plan is your pic

62382793
>>62382788
I find this pick really depressing because it reminds me of my parents. They are just in another fucking world. You cannot convince them that anything is wrong.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382840
>>62382768
Probably because houses and companies have value while crypto is useless and does nothing at all

62382853
>buy bitcoin 2015
>???
>buy house

what was so difficult?

62382958
>>62382765
The sad thing is that the pod in the pic related doesn't even have to say "MAiD" on it for everyone to know what it's supposed to be.
>>62382788
I'm convinced MAID is some kind of scam to prevent wealth transfer from boomers to their kids; there's no way it could enjoy as broad support amongst boomers (leaf boomers are the most selfish on the planet) if that wasn't the case.
Probably the idea is spend everything, leave nothing, and jump in the death pod when you're tapped out.

62383964
>>62382765
They didn't plan to ever pay it off, inflation eats away at it and they'll roll it into their next house.

Or at least that was the plan before the bubble popped and they all went $200k-500k underwater.

62383966
400K CAD is something like 250K EUR

62383978
Just rent?

62383992
>>62383966
The median household income in canada is like 40k euro. People have no idea how fucked this country is, they're still going off of marketing from 20 years ago.

62384000
>>62383992
I know i was working remotely for a company in Quebec and we all got laid off 3 months ago

62384029
>>62382768
They won't let it crash. BC has gotten rid of all businesses since it's bad for environment and were living on the housing market and US tech sector. Now both is gone so fed need to bail them out. We've already seen them changing the laws so you can't force sell because you're missing mortgage.
No wonder Alberta had enough.

62384087
The market was supposed to crash in 2023/2024, but the government flooded the country with immigrants to keep prices high

62384116
>>62383964
Did it pop?

62384139
>>62382765
Paying it down makes you poor.

62384170
>>62382765
Housing-as-investment needs to crash and die off permanently. So much wealth and productivity is wasted on paying for retardedly overpriced nonproductive shitshacks. Pricegouging wooden boxes just kills the rest of the economy and makes people stop giving a fuck about anything.

62384294
>>62384170
Any kind of land-as-investmen needs to be regulated to hell. There's downtown office space in Toronto that has been sitting empty for YEARS. You're telling me no one wants to open a business in downtown Toronto in YEARS? Fuck land hoarders.
